message.content:
You are executing a NuGet Central Package Management (CPM) migration for the repo `data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality` on GitLab at `gitlab.rivagecapital.com`. Follow these steps exactly.

## Environment
- Windows; Bash = Git Bash. `glab` authenticated; `git`, `dotnet`, `jq` available.
- Scratchpad: `C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad`
- Do NOT touch `C:\Code\dterziev\CoreClaw`

## Step 1: Clone the repo and create branch

```bash
cd "C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad"
glab repo clone data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality -- --depth 1 -b master
cd coremont.snap.dataquality
git checkout -b chore/adopt-cpm
```

## Step 2: Capture baseline build

Run:
```bash
dotnet restore
dotnet build -bl:"C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad\snap-dataquality-before.binlog" -c Release
```

If restore/build fails due to private feed/credentials → record CI-ONLY and skip local validation (steps 6-8). If it fails for another reason, stop and investigate.

## Step 3: Audit package references

Before running the conversion, audit the packages:
```bash
dotnet list package --format json > "C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad\baseline-packages.json"
```

Also check for existing `Directory.Packages.props` and `packages.config` files. If any `packages.config` found, STOP and report BLOCKED.

## Step 4: Perform CPM conversion

Consolidation policy: **highest version per package**; major version jumps → use per-project `VersionOverride` (list them). Transitive pinning OFF.

Manually perform the conversion:

1. Create `Directory.Packages.props` in the repo root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` set to `true` and one `<PackageVersion>` per unique package (alphabetically sorted), using the highest version found across all projects.

2. For any package where different projects use different **major** versions, keep the lower version as a `VersionOverride` in the specific project file instead of bumping it centrally.

3. Strip `Version="..."` from every `<PackageReference>` in every `.csproj`/`.fsproj`/`.vbproj`. If a project needs a version different from the central one (due to major version conflict), add `VersionOverride="X.Y.Z"` to that `<PackageReference>`.

4. If `Directory.Build.props` exists and contains package version properties, inline them into `Directory.Packages.props`.

## Step 5: Patch Dockerfiles (REQUIRED - systematic CI break prevention)

Inspect every Dockerfile in the repo (`Dockerfile`, `build.Dockerfile`, `*.Dockerfile`, etc.).

For each Dockerfile that has a layered restore pattern — meaning it explicitly copies files like `COPY Directory.Build.props ./` and then runs `dotnet restore` BEFORE `COPY . ./`:
- If it copies `Directory.Build.props` explicitly by name (not via `COPY *.props ./`), add immediately after that line: `COPY Directory.Packages.props ./`
- If it already uses `COPY *.props ./` glob, no change needed — it already picks up the new file.

Do NOT patch Dockerfiles that restore after `COPY . ./` (full source copy) or that build FROM a base image with `--no-restore`.

## Step 6: Validate

Run post-conversion build if baseline was successful (not CI-ONLY):
```bash
dotnet list package --format json > "C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad\after-cpm-packages.json"
dotnet restore
dotnet build -bl:"C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad\snap-dataquality-after.binlog" -c Release
dotnet test -c Release
```

## Step 7: Commit

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: adopt Central Package Management"
```

## Step 8: Create tracking issue on Clarion/data

Create an issue describing the problem (no CPM, version drift risk) and why it matters:
```bash
glab issue create --repo Clarion/data \
  --title "Adopt Central Package Management in coremont.snap.dataquality" \
  --assignee bhirani \
  --description "..."
```

## Step 9: Push branch and open MR

Push the branch and open MR in the TARGET repo (not Clarion/data), ready (not draft), assigned to bhirani, with --remove-source-branch:
```bash
git push -u origin chore/adopt-cpm
glab mr create --repo data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality \
  --source-branch chore/adopt-cpm \
  --target-branch master \
  --remove-source-branch \
  --assignee bhirani \
  --title "chore: adopt Central Package Management" \
  --description "..."
```

Then add a cross-link note on the tracking issue pointing to the MR URL.

## Step 10: Check CI pipeline

After opening the MR, wait a moment then check for a pipeline:
```bash
glab mr view <MR_IID> --repo data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality -F json | jq '.pipeline'
```

Or use:
```bash
glab pipeline list --repo data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality --per-page 3 -F json | jq '.[0]'
```

Report the pipeline/build job URL and status.

## Report back

Return these results:
- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED
- Issue URL (full https)
- MR URL (full https)  
- Validation: local-build+tests | CI-only (+ reason)
- Dockerfile patched? yes/no (which files)
- CI build job status (past restore? job URL)
- Consolidation: # packages centralized; any VersionOverrides + reason
- Concerns

## IMPORTANT notes:
- Use Bash tool (Git Bash syntax) for all commands
- All paths use forward slashes or quoted Windows paths
- `glab` is on PATH and authenticated
- The scratchpad directory is: `C:\Users\bhirani\AppData\Local\Temp\3\claude\c--Code-dterziev-CoreClaw\e2f6752b-555b-4db9-ab95-752506132736\scratchpad`
- In GitLab issue/MR descriptions: use repo-qualified refs with trailing + (e.g. `data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality!1+`)
- In replies to user: use full https URLs

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.text: Now create the tracking issue on Clarion/data:

input.command:
glab issue create --repo Clarion/data \
  --title "Adopt Central Package Management in coremont.snap.dataquality" \
  --assignee bhirani \
  --description "## Problem

The \`coremont.snap.dataquality\` repo manages NuGet package versions individually across \`Directory.Build.props\`, \`tests/Directory.Build.props\`, and five \`.csproj\` files. This creates version drift risk — the same package can appear at different versions in different projects with no enforcement mechanism.

Before this change, the repo had a live example of drift: \`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk\`, \`NUnit\`, \`NUnit3TestAdapter\`, and \`coverlet.collector\` were each declared at two different versions across the test \`Directory.Build.props\` and \`Coremont.Snap.Tests.csproj\`, producing NU1504 duplicate-reference warnings on every restore.

## Why it matters

Without CPM, version inconsistencies are invisible until they surface as runtime errors or unexpected behaviour. CPM enforces a single source of truth for all package versions, making audits and upgrades straightforward.

## Solution

Implemented via data-services/data-development/coremont.snap.dataquality!1+ — adopts \`ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true\` with a new \`Directory.Packages.props\` at the repo root, strips \`Version=\` from all \`PackageReference\` items, and removes the duplicate references that caused the pre-existing NU1504 warning." 2>&1
